summary: Clear soundboard capture of a solid ’74 run, highlighted by an epic Dark Star → Morning Dew sequence and strong second‑set jams.
review: The Miller soundboard source delivers a generally clear and well‑balanced mix, despite occasional SQ hiccups and crowd bleed at the end. Musically the show shines on the second‑set space journey (Seastones‑Jam‑Dark Star‑Morning Dew) and a high‑energy third set featuring New Fat ... , One More Saturday Night and U.S. Blues, while a few slower numbers (Sugaree, Peggy‑O, Bertha) fall flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and praise the performance, especially the Dark Star/Morning Dew jam; recording is described as clear and balanced with only minor issues, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced; occasional SQ problems (muffled sections, drop‑outs, extra crowd noise) noted but tolerable. Miller mix praised for clarity over other sources.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts a bit slow but gains momentum with high‑octane numbers; second set is the highlight, a classic space jam anchored by Seastones and Dark Star; third set mixes solid rockers with some uneven moments, though GDTRFB and the closing trio lift it.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone praised on Dark Star, Morning Dew and GSET; leads the space jam with confidence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Heavy “bombs” noted during the Morning Dew section; overall strong playing throughout the jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir delivers solid rhythm guitar and vocal work, especially on Jack Straw and One More Saturday Night.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (keyboard) mainly on piano rather than organ for this show; no major complaints.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Billy) highlighted for “perfected” drumming on Let It Grow and strong overall groove.
